specification.topic-statement: The separator layer renders a single-row horizontal dividing line with optional embedded navigation context and optional embedded debug information.

scope.in-scope: Separator layer creation, visibility determination, height reporting, the rendering algorithm including baseline line characters, navigation context formatting, debug information formatting, visual-width-aware layout, and the post-line terminal sequence.
scope.boundary: The separator participates in the general layer rendering pipeline through the standard visibility, height, and render callbacks. How the pipeline invokes those callbacks and assembles the overall frame is out of scope.

Computing the number of terminal columns occupied by a byte sequence, excluding ANSI escape sequences, is performed by the scrollback display-width utility. This specification records that the utility is used to measure navigation and debug string widths, not how the utility operates.

The parent, sibling, and current agent UUIDs and child count are supplied by the caller through a post-creation configuration step. How those values are determined (agent registry lookups, tree traversal) is out of scope.

field.name: visible flag
field.note: raw pointer; visibility is determined by dereferencing this pointer at render time, not at creation time

field.name: debug info
field.note: scroll offset, first visible row, terminal height, document height (all size_t pointers), and render elapsed time (uint64_t pointer); all default to null at creation; all must be set together via a dedicated configuration call to enable debug display

field.name: navigation context
field.type: struct of optional strings and count
field.note: parent UUID, previous sibling UUID, current UUID, next sibling UUID (all const char pointers, nullable), and child count (size_t); all default to null/zero at creation; set via a dedicated configuration call

field.name: parent indicator
field.note: upward arrow followed by first 6 characters of parent UUID followed by '...' when parent UUID is non-null; dim upward arrow followed by '-' (ANSI reset after) when parent UUID is null

field.name: previous sibling indicator
field.note: leftward arrow followed by first 6 characters of previous sibling UUID followed by '...' when non-null; dim leftward arrow followed by '-' (ANSI reset after) when null

field.name: current agent
field.note: '[' followed by first 6 characters of current UUID followed by '...]'

field.name: next sibling indicator
field.note: rightward arrow followed by first 6 characters of next sibling UUID followed by '...' when non-null; dim rightward arrow followed by '-' (ANSI reset after) when null

field.name: child count indicator
field.note: downward arrow followed by the decimal child count when count is greater than zero; dim downward arrow followed by '-' (ANSI reset after) when count is zero

field.name: off
field.note: current scroll offset; the viewport_offset pointer value, or 0 if pointer is null

field.name: row
field.note: first visible row; the viewport_row pointer value, or 0 if pointer is null

field.name: h
field.note: terminal height in rows; the viewport_height pointer value, or 0 if pointer is null

field.name: doc
field.note: total document height; the document_height pointer value, or 0 if pointer is null

field.name: sb
field.note: scrollback rows; computed as document_height minus 3 when document_height is at least 3, otherwise 0; the subtracted 3 accounts for the separator, input, and status layers

field.name: t
field.note: render elapsed time: formatted as an integer followed by 'us' when elapsed is below 1000 microseconds; formatted as a decimal with one decimal place followed by 'ms' when elapsed is 1000 microseconds or above

behavior.creation: The separator layer is created with a name string and a pointer to an external boolean visibility flag. Debug info pointers are all initialized to null. Navigation context strings are all initialized to null and child count to zero. The layer registers three callbacks: visibility check, height query, and render.
behavior.visibility: The visibility predicate dereferences the stored boolean pointer and returns its value. The flag is not copied at creation; changes to the pointed-to boolean are reflected immediately at the next render.
behavior.height: The separator always reports a height of exactly one row regardless of terminal width or content.
behavior.rendering:
Rendering produces exactly one row by the following algorithm:
If the current agent UUID is non-null, a navigation string is assembled by formatting all five navigation elements (parent indicator, previous sibling indicator, current agent, next sibling indicator, child count indicator) with single spaces separating them and a leading and trailing space, in that order. The resulting string is stored in a fixed 256-byte buffer.
If the current agent UUID is null, no navigation string is produced and the navigation string has zero length.
If the debug scroll-offset pointer is non-null, a debug string is assembled containing all six fields in the format described in the data contracts section. The time threshold for switching from microsecond to millisecond display is exactly 1000 microseconds (values of 999 and below use the microsecond form; values of 1000 and above use the millisecond form). The debug string is stored in a fixed 128-byte buffer with a leading and trailing space.
If the debug scroll-offset pointer is null, no debug string is produced and the debug string has zero length.
The visual widths of the navigation and debug strings are computed by scanning their bytes and excluding all ANSI escape code sequences. The total number of horizontal box-drawing characters to emit is the terminal width minus the sum of both visual widths. If the sum of visual widths is zero or equals or exceeds the terminal width, the full terminal width of box-drawing characters is emitted.
Navigation strings contain ANSI dim and reset sequences for absent-slot indicators. These sequences consume bytes but occupy no terminal columns. Using display-width calculation rather than byte length ensures the total rendered row width matches the terminal width exactly.
The row is assembled by appending to the output buffer in this order:
1. The computed number of horizontal box-drawing characters (Unicode U+2500, three bytes each in UTF-8), one at a time. 2. The navigation string, if non-empty. 3. The debug string, if non-empty. 4. A clear-to-end-of-line sequence followed by carriage return and line feed.
behavior.post-creation-configuration:
Two separate post-creation operations update the layer's state:
The debug configuration operation accepts five pointers and stores all of them, replacing any previously stored pointers. All five are expected to be set together; the debug display is gated solely on whether the scroll-offset pointer is non-null.
The navigation context configuration operation accepts four UUID string pointers and a child count value, storing them directly as raw pointers. The stored pointers alias the caller's strings and are not copied. Any or all UUID pointers may be null.
Both debug pointers and navigation UUID pointers are raw aliases to memory owned elsewhere. The layer does not copy or free them. Callers must ensure the pointed-to data remains valid for the layer's lifetime.
